title: Game Programming Patterns
creator: Robert Nystrom
description: A freely readable book explaining reusable software patterns through game-oriented examples, including loops, components, events, data locality, and object pools.
useful: A bridge between general software-pattern vocabulary and the particular constraints of real-time games.
audience: Intermediate programmers who know basic code and want stronger game architecture vocabulary.
learning: Recognize recurring structures in game code
Compare patterns by cost and appropriate use
Apply events, components, loops, and data locality
